title: Tangy Flaky Ethiopian Tofu Flatbread
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish with a twist, featuring marinated tofu and a flaky flatbread, perfect for a nostalgic and flavorful meal. The combination of soya milk and Mediterranean spices creates a unique and delicious taste experience.

ingredients.name: extra-firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soya milk, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, ginger, and berbere spice.
Add the cubed tofu to the marinade and mix well to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or overnight.
In a separate large bowl, combine flour, salt, and yeast.
Gradually add warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces. Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Place a piece of the dough on the skillet and top with a few pieces of the marinated tofu.
Fold the dough over the tofu to form a half-moon shape and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining dough and tofu.
